title: Top Five Movie Soundtrack Needle Drops. Plus: Robbie Robertson
format: Sean and Ringer staff writer Rob Harvilla trade top-five needle drops, then Robbie Robertson on 'Once Were Brothers' and scoring movies with Scorsese.

segments.heading: What makes a good needle drop

films.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
films.note: Rob name-checks Shazamming a song mid-screening as basically his whole relationship to movie music.

films.title: Fifty Shades of Grey
films.note: Cited for the slowed-down, ironic trailer-cover trend, alongside Suicide Squad.

films.title: Suicide Squad
films.note: Same trailer-cover trend Rob says goes too far into pure irony.

films.title: Dazed and Confused
films.note: Brought up for Linklater's rule of using music teenagers actually listened to, not what a later generation assumes they did.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Sean notes Tarantino has said many of its songs came straight off the KHJ playlist of the period.

segments.heading: Sean and Rob's top five needle drops
segments.note: counted down five to one, one host per pick

picks.title: Django Unchained
picks.note: No. 5: Rick Ross's '100 Black Coffins' plays as Django rides free, his one respite in what he calls his least favorite Tarantino movie.

picks.title: Groundhog Day
picks.note: No. 5: Sonny & Cher's 'I Got You Babe' as the alarm-clock reset. He dislikes the song but says it does exactly what it needs to do to Bill Murray.

picks.title: Chungking Express
picks.note: No. 4: 'California Dreamin'' on repeat in Wong Kar-wai's U.S. breakout, boosted stateside by a Tarantino-endorsed Miramax release.

picks.title: Fight Club
picks.note: No. 4: the Pixies' 'Where Is My Mind' over the buildings falling. He says the movie turned a deep cut into a canonical hit.

picks.title: The Royal Tenenbaums
picks.note: No. 3: Nico's 'These Days' as Gwyneth Paltrow steps off the bus toward Luke Wilson.

picks.title: Do the Right Thing
picks.note: No. 3: Public Enemy's 'Fight the Power' under Rosie Perez's opening-credits dance, which he calls the most significant needle drop of the last 30-40 years.

picks.title: Goodfellas
picks.note: No. 2: Derek and the Dominos' 'Layla' piano coda scoring the bodies turning up after the Lufthansa heist.

picks.title: Reservoir Dogs
picks.note: No. 2: Stealers Wheel's 'Stuck in the Middle with You' under Michael Madsen's ear-cutting dance.

picks.title: Wayne's World
picks.note: No. 1: Queen's 'Bohemian Rhapsody' headbanged in the car, in a movie directed by Penelope Spheeris.

picks.title: Mean Streets
picks.note: No. 1: the Rolling Stones' 'Jumpin' Jack Flash' scoring De Niro's entrance, the moment he says Scorsese invented the modern needle drop.

picks.title: This Is Spinal Tap
picks.note: The a cappella, out-of-key 'Heartbreak Hotel' at Elvis's grave.

picks.title: High Fidelity
picks.note: The Beta Band record-store scene: 'I will now sell five copies of the Three EP.'

picks.title: Animal House
picks.note: Belushi smashing the folk singer's guitar on the stairs.

picks.title: Reality Bites
picks.note: 'My Sharona' turning a convenience store into a dance party.

picks.title: Almost Famous
picks.note: The tour-bus 'Tiny Dancer' sing-along, left off the list proper for being too well-worn by now.

picks.title: Billy Madison
picks.note: ELO's 'Telephone Line' under Steve Buscemi's murder-list phone call.

picks.title: Lost in Translation
picks.note: The Jesus and Mary Chain's 'Just Like Honey' closing the film.

picks.title: American Psycho
picks.note: Huey Lewis's 'Hip to Be Square' right before Christian Bale starts killing people.

picks.title: Beetlejuice
picks.note: Harry Belafonte's 'Day-O' dinner-party possession scene.

picks.title: Boogie Nights
picks.note: Night Ranger's 'Sister Christian' scoring the Alfred Molina firecracker scene; Sean's own alternate pick from the movie is Charles Wright's 'Do Your Thing.'

films.title: Once Were Brothers: Robbie Robertson and the Band
films.note: The documentary drawn from his memoir Testimony; Martin Scorsese came on as executive producer after seeing a rough cut.

films.title: The Last Waltz
films.note: The Band's farewell concert film, which he says was more a performance document than the full history 'Once Were Brothers' tells.

films.title: Easy Rider
films.note: Where he says a lot of people first heard 'The Weight.'

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: His latest Scorsese scoring job; some of that work found its way onto his new record, 'Sinematic.'

films.title: Double Indemnity
films.note: One of the film noirs that hooked him young. He says he's 'never gotten over' it.

films.title: Kiss of Death
films.note: Another early noir touchstone he names alongside Double Indemnity.

films.title: 8½
films.note: The Fellini film he saw in a theater between sets, after which he says he was never the same.

films.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
films.note: Howlin' Wolf's 'Spoonful' scoring Belfort's walk down the street, a deliberate mismatch he calls 'pure magic.'

films.title: Casino
films.note: Another Scorsese collaboration he singles out for its music, including a Devo cue.

films.title: Jojo Rabbit
films.note: One of the last great movies he'd seen, alongside Parasite.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: The other film he names when asked for the last great thing he's seen.

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood

Once Were Brothers: Robbie Robertson and.title: Once Were Brothers: Robbie Robertson and the Band
